D7 Print L5 Evaluate Reference

ISO 12647 & Fogra Tolerances

a print is "to standard" when every measured patch sits inside its reference tolerance — sheet-fed offset is tight, newsprint is loose

ISO 12647 defines reference Lab values for the CMYK solids and their overprints on each paper class, plus the ΔE tolerance each measured patch must stay within. Fogra publishes the characterisation data (FOGRA51, FOGRA39, FOGRA52) that supplies those reference values. Press variability scatters measured patches around the references; a print passes when every one is inside its ring.

Pick a standard's tolerance class and push press variability. Sheet-fed offset on coated stock (tight) breaks first; newsprint's looser tolerance forgives much more drift. The readout counts how many of the eight reference patches stay inside their rings.
